item.title: Zelenskyy's dismissal of Ukrainian defence minister sparks public row, protests
item.description: A dispute between the now former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov and Kyiv's top general is spilling into public, after Volodymyr Zelenskyy orders a second reshuffle of this year.

item.title: Mike Connor visited the same park for 40 years. His notebooks filled with birds are invaluable
item.description: Mike Connor visited the same park for 40 years and wrote down what he saw. He didn't realise he was incidentally chronicling a changing environment and contributing to science.

item.title: In Tokyo, a manga by a convicted cannibal made me question everything
item.description: In a rare bookstore in Japan, I find a manga written by a convicted cannibal. It becomes the subject of a moral dilemma I've been grappling with as a filmmaker.

item.title: ABC news quiz: The iconic film Sam Neill rejected, England's World Cup misery, a royal reunion and a mystery outbreak
item.description: Our weekly quiz has the film Sam Neill "didn't think was funny", plus the sweetness at the centre of our galaxy.

item.title: Alleged DV victim labelled 'difficult witness', family court permanently removes children
item.description: The Family Court granted John full custody of his kids, but he returned them to their mother, saying the judge got it wrong.

item.title: Dr Morag MacSween is calling on the court to improve its understanding of perpetrator tactics.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: These tankers chose a Strait of Hormuz route, triggered Iran and lit up the Gulf
item.description: The commercial tankers struck as they transited the Strait of Hormuz in recent weeks were not random, but part of a "highly, highly targeted" campaign run by Tehran, according to marine traffic watchers.

item.title: Workers' rights to drive AI debate at Labor conference, insiders say
item.description: Labor insiders expect ongoing debate about artificial intelligence when party faithful gather to hash out a policy platform to guide the next three years.

item.title: The zeitgeist doesn't suit Angus Taylor but he could do more to help himself
item.description: The mismatch between where the public resides and what the Liberals are expounding is a deeper challenge — a gulf perhaps unable to be bridged, at least in the short term.

item.title: Vaping rates among younger Australians plunge as smoking falls to historic low
item.description: Vaping and smoking rates are on the decline, but a shift to other nicotine products could represent an increase in average use.

item.title: South Korea wants to be a top-three AI superpower. Here's why memory chips are key
item.description: South Korea is doubling down on its bid to lead in AI, unveiling plans last month to spend hundreds of billions on developing its chip manufacturing.

item.title: Former diplomat says Telstra 'priority' service failures putting wife's life at risk
item.description: Telstra promised Alastair Gaisford that his wife's healthcare was a priority. But he says 15 landline outages, including some that could have prevented the ability to call Triple Zero in an emergency, have convinced him otherwise.

item.title: Judicial watchdog directs County Court judge towards counselling after 'unfair and inappropriate' comments
item.description: A Victorian judge who admonished a barrister in front of her client will receive counselling and training, following a ruling by the judicial watchdog.

item.title: Future of Liberty Bell Bay site in doubt following manganese smelter's closure
item.description: Australia's only manganese alloy smelter has closed, leaving the future of the site uncertain and the country more reliant on imported alloys for steel production.

item.title: Man charged with murder after allegedly setting father on fire at Glenmore Park
item.description: A man who allegedly set his father on fire and left him to die outside a home in Sydney's west has been charged with murder.

item.title: Dealers warn EV drivers of repair and refund delays under warranty from new brands
item.description: As an influx of car brands enters the market, dealers ask for a change to consumer laws to fix a grey area that makes them responsible for warranty repairs.

item.title: Gusty winds to hit south-east Queensland as far north braces for cold blast
item.description: A combination of two strong weather systems will bring wind and rain to south-east Queensland, while residents further north could see the mercury dip to single digits.

item.title: Opals master coach Sandy Brondello turns to 'different style' in bid to match USA dominance
item.description: No country has been able to topple the United States at either the FIBA World Cup or the Olympics since 2006. Here's what Sandy Brondello is doing to try to get Australia's Opals over the hump.

item.title: What is pneumonia? The common infection that caused Sam Neill's death
item.description: After the actor's death from pneumonia, here's what to know about the common infection, its symptoms and why it can sometimes become deadly.

item.title: Natasha never expected to become addicted to prescribed opioids while battling endometriosis
item.description: Natasha Trajkovski never imagined her prescribed opioid medication to help with the pain of endometriosis would lead her to addiction.

item.title: ATO questioned over $73m move into controversial Adelaide office tower
item.description: The Australian Taxation Office will spend at least $73.4 million to relocate its offices into "the best building in Adelaide", four streets away.

item.title: Horsham council rejects mining company WIM Resource's invitation to join reference group
item.description: A regional council has declined a mining company's invitation to join a community reference group and slammed the company for its attitude towards community concerns.

item.title: RSV vaccination rates halve NSW hospitalisations, as influenza peak looms
item.description: New South Wales health authorities have hailed a vaccination program that has nearly halved hospitalisations for a potentially deadly respiratory virus among newborn babies a "fantastic" success, as the winter illness peak fast approaches.

item.title: Driver flips car during police chase in Melbourne's north-east
item.description: Police arrest a driver and his passenger after a high-speed chase through Melbourne's north-eastern suburbs.

item.title: Apollo boilerplate to be centrepiece of Powerhouse Parramatta's opening exhibition
item.description: It took 43 days and a custom cage to bring the 4.5-tonne test command module from Washington to Castle Hill in Sydney's north-west.

item.title: Ultra-low tides bring rare chance to walk to an island lighthouse
item.description: A rare chance to walk 1 kilometre offshore to an island lighthouse attracts thousands of trekkers to a North Queensland town.

item.title: As it happened: Big miners BHP and Rio Tinto dive as investors fret about investment outlook
item.description: BHP Group's investors are in a world of pain today. It follows Thursday's operational update from the miner.

item.title: Uber removes 'unacceptable' surcharge for Assist users after outcry
item.description: Uber has reversed its decision to impose a $5 fee on passengers requiring extra assistance, after advocates and service providers labelled it discriminatory and a "disability tax".

item.title: Groundwater concerns grow as state pushes ahead with Taroom Trough oil plans
item.description: An expert is warning that the push to fast-track approvals for the proposed Taroom Trough lacks crucial detail.

item.title: Ngura Kutju set up in Alice Springs to advocate for Indigenous families in NPY Lands
item.description: A new hub to support families caught between three child protection agencies has been established in Alice Springs in a 12-month trial.

item.title: Three men charged over alleged Coles Bay robbery and arson in which man had to watch home burn down
item.description: Three men are charged in separate Launceston suburbs over an alleged violent armed robbery and house fire at Coles Bay, on Tasmania's east coast.

item.title: When is the World Cup final? How to watch Spain vs Argentina
item.description: The World Cup final is nearly upon us, with Spain to face Argentina for the ultimate prize. Here's everything you need to know about the biggest game in football.

item.title: Spain trains in 'hazardous' air ahead of World Cup final with Argentina
item.description: World Cup finalists Spain train in less-than-ideal conditions in New Jersey as wildfires cause excessive smoke in the north-east of the United States.

item.title: Central Queensland residents warn of fire risks on overgrown crown land
item.description: A central Queensland man is concerned his neighbour's overgrown plot is a fire hazard. His neighbour is the state government.

item.title: Trump claims China acquired personal information of 220m US voters — as it happened
item.description: Donald Trump claims declassified documents reveal China carried out the "largest compromise of election data in history".

item.title: Destructive cyclone season has communities in northern WA still cleaning up
item.description: Two cyclones in less than a month caused significant damage to the Dampier Peninsula in the Kimberley region during the 2025-26 wet season. Months later, residents say costly repairs are continuing.

item.title: Mark and Michelle Jones warn of the dangers of methanol poisoning
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Daryl Maguire found guilty of conspiracy over fraudulent visa scheme
item.description: After five days of deliberation in the NSW District Court the former Member for Wagga Wagga was found to have conspired to provide false or misleading information to the Commonwealth on visas for Chinese nationals seeking permanent residency.

item.title: Ancient trade fast evaporating as distillers call for coopering skills to be better preserved
item.description: This ancient trade of coopering is fast evaporating as distillers look abroad to help keep their flavours top shelf.

item.title: Queensland hospital settles dispute with psychiatrist Dr Jillian Spencer over gender care
item.description: Dr Jillian Spencer has ended her employment with Queensland Children's Hospital after reaching a confidential settlement with Children’s Health Queensland, bringing an end to a three-year dispute.

item.title: British government urges FIFA to take action against Argentina over Falklands banner
item.description: The British government urges FIFA to investigate after Argentine players held up a banner declaring the Falkland Islands were Argentinian.

item.title: Corum Ash discharged from mental health hospital months before deadly Yennora attack, prompting review
item.description: Corum Ash was released from a mental health hospital months before he allegedly went on to kill a 74-year-old Sydney father of six.

item.title: Man charged over alleged assaults at Melbourne massage parlours
item.description: A 32-year-old man was arrested after video footage of the alleged assaults were shared on social media.

item.title: From sports comedies to Coober Pedy mystery, here's what to watch in July
item.description: Plus a noir mystery set in outback Coober Pedy, a true crime drama which doesn't exploit its victims, and more new TV to watch this month.

item.title: Damien Leith reflects on friendship with Roy Orbison's co-songwriter
item.description: Every Monday morning for the last 14 years, Australian musician Damien Leith has been meeting with the late Joe Melson, the Nashville songwriter responsible for some of Roy Orbison's biggest hits.

item.title: Smoke from Canada wildfires crosses into US prompting warnings to stay indoors
item.description: Heavy, pungent wildfire smoke darkens skies in the US from the Great Lakes to parts of the East Coast, reducing visibility and prompting warnings that breathing the air outside could be dangerous.

item.title: Rohan Dennis pleads guilty to driving while banned for five years over crash that killed wife
item.description: Former professional cyclist and two-time Olympian Rohan Dennis pleads guilty after he was caught driving while disqualified, not far from where his wife died two-and-a-half years ago.

item.title: Josh Fawaz's Like a Prayer now has AI credits on Spotify
item.description: DJ and music producer Josh Fawaz has updated the song credits on Spotify on his viral track Like a Prayer. Generative AI drums and vocals are now credited.

item.title: Telstra admits accountability for national outage during Senate inquiry
item.description: The national Telstra outage last week was triggered by maintenance work that caused the system's clocks to go back two decades, the telco says.

item.title: Mount Isa Police allegedly seize $165,000 worth of methamphetamine from outback home
item.description: Police allege more than 200 grams of methamphetamines have been seized during a drug bust at a Mount Isa home in outback Queensland.

item.title: US strike hits strategic bridge in southern Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Trump rails against US voting systems in prime-time speech ahead of critical midterms
item.description: US President Donald Trump uses a presidential speech to accuse China of improperly trying to influence the 2020 election and stop him from winning the presidency.

item.title: US strikes bridges and collapses a tower at a key port as its Iran campaign expands
item.description: The United States expanded its air strike campaign against Iran early Friday by hitting more bridges and apparently collapsing a tower at a key Iranian port.

item.title: The main culprit of Australia's productivity crisis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How the Hawks are making their two-ruck strategy work in the 2026 AFL season
item.description: Once thought to be dead and buried, the era of the dual ruck has been resuscitated by a handful of clubs in finals contention, including Hawthorn.

item.title: US claims strikes on Iranian targets
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Supreme Court battle over traditional owner consultation pauses flood works at Nyah
item.description: The Victorian government and a regional water authority have admitted they did not property consult with traditional owners before approving a controversial flood plain restoration project.

item.title: What's living in your make-up brushes and is it harmful?
item.description: Various studies have shown that make-up tools, including brushes, can harbour a range of bacteria, including opportunistic pathogens.

item.title: Bus driver found guilty over Brisbane CBD crash that killed Tia Cameron appeals conviction
item.description: Lindsay Francis Selby was found guilty last year of driving without due care and attention when he struck and killed Tia Cameron in 2024.

item.title: Royal commissions might 'fund lawyers' but violence experts want to fund solutions
item.description: Experts are divided about whether there should be a royal commission into violence against women but say the solutions to the problem are already known and need funding.

item.title: SA Police report man for driving homemade car in the Riverland
item.description: The disqualified driver had made the car from a gopher mobility scooter and the body of a child's ride-on car, and will appear in court at a later date.

item.title: Donald Trump's failure to let go of 2020 an 'ominous sign' ahead of upcoming midterm elections
item.description: Using a prime-time address to the nation to repeat long-debunked claims about the 2020 election defeat is a desperate and unusual move. Analysts say it's an "ominous sign" of how Donald Trump may handle the upcoming midterms.

item.title: NSW woman arrested as investigation into police killer Dezi Freeman's movements continues
item.description: A NSW woman was arrested in Bairnsdale today as part of Victoria Police's investigation into the movements of dead police killer Dezi Freeman.

item.title: New Zealand detects first case of a native bird with deadly H5N1 bird flu
item.description: Days after a migratory bird tested positive for bird flu in Wellington, authorities have detected a second case of the deadly virus in a native bird.

item.title: New report lists Melbourne and Sydney as world's most expensive cities for beer and cigarettes
item.description: The Mapping the World's Prices report, published by Deutsche Bank, compares cost-of-living data in 69 cities across six continents.

item.title: Storm's injury crisis deepens as star's season ends in loss to Roosters
item.description: Will Warbrick's Melbourne Storm career appears to be over, with the departing winger rupturing his Achilles in his side's 14-6 loss to the Sydney Roosters.

item.title: Mount Gambier man admits to indecent behaviour at third shop in April
item.description: A Mount Gambier man who previously admitted going naked in two local food businesses pleads guilty to another charge of indecent behaviour, as his case heads to the new South Australian Veterans Court.

item.title: Holiday care worker charged with child abuse also worked in NT public school
item.description: The ABC can reveal a man charged with sexually abusing a "very young child" at a greater Darwin school holiday program had also recently worked at a Top End school.

item.title: Stepson sentenced to nine years in jail over mummified body found in Gold Coast family home
item.description: Nikola Golem lied to his "mentally unwell" mother about her husband's death for more than a year, and let her sleep beside the decomposing body, Brisbane's Supreme Court heard.

item.title: Truck driver, 33, charged over Guildford train crash that injured three people
item.description: The 33-year-old driver is accused of driving dangerously and driving with an illicit drug in his system in a crash that injured three people.

item.title: Star Swan takes MOTY contender as Crows notch vital win
item.description: The Adelaide Crows beat the Swans in a thriller in the wet at the SCG to jump into the top four.

item.title: Friday Finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Andy Burnham to become next UK PM after British-Australian candidate gets one vote
item.description: Andy Burnham will be Britain's seventh prime minister in 10 years next week after winning a Labour leadership contest in which his only challenger, an Australian, received just one vote.

item.title: Lao authorities charge distillery owners over fatal methanol poisoning, Danish officials confirm
item.description: Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles, both 19, died after the incident at the Nana Backpackers Hostel in Vang Vieng in November 2024.

item.title: Ukraine destroys strategic bomber aircraft at Russia's Engels airbase, Zelenskyy says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said the military plane was used by Russia for missile strikes on Ukraine.

item.title: Cricket legend Sir Garfield Sobers dies aged 89
item.description: Sir Don Bradman once described Sobers as "the greatest all-round cricketer I ever saw".

item.title: From Book Doof to bedazzling, social reading is all the rage
item.description: People find community with other bookworms as social reading takes off with everything from literature raves to book bedazzling.

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Sam Neill's Marvel cameo, 70s punk rock and numbat tongues
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right, the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Australia's newest medical drama The F Ward, starring Ioane Sa'ula, mixes Bump with Grey's Anatomy
item.description: Growing up, Ioane Sa'ula thought he'd probably play rugby league professionally, but an injury scuppered his plans. Now, he's the romantic lead of new Aussie medical drama, The F Ward.

item.title: Battery installation rates collapse as 'solar-coaster' strikes back
item.description: Federal subsidies have ignited huge demand for batteries, but a tightening of the rules has experts warning of "boom and bust" cycles for the industry.

item.title: Oil-rich Gulf nations building alternatives to Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Amid uncertainty around the Strait of Hormuz, for months Gulf countries and oil companies have been finding ways to bypass the choke point.

item.title: Shortage of refined fuels threatening global economy
item.description: There is a rapidly escalating supply crunch that has yet to show up on oil markets. While the price of crude has risen in the past week as the fragile Iran war ceasefire has crumbled, the remarkably restrained price moves have helped create a false sense of security.

item.title: One Nation's Malcolm Roberts falsely claimed COVID-19 vaccines caused miscarriages
item.description: The One Nation senator made the previously unreported remarks during a live stream discussion with three prominent Australian anti-vaccine mandate activists.

item.title: From scream king to COVID dad: The on and off-screen faces of Sam Neill
item.description: The world has been remembering Sam Neill's incredible film and television career in the wake of the actor's death, but his off-screen antics left a robust impact.

item.title: Want to know who you'll marry? Science says you should look in the mirror
item.description: Once I saw it, I couldn't unsee it. Had I noticed something real or was it just the people around me? I set out to find out what the data had to say.

item.title: Prospects for Palestinian statehood 'disappearing', former world leaders say
item.description: The two former world leaders are demanding the international community heap more pressure on Israel to change course.

item.title: Federal racism inquiry hears of racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence
item.description: Racial slurs, online trolls and fears of far-right violence have taken a toll on First Nations people, a federal inquiry heard this week.

item.title: Minister concedes big tech companies using Australian content to train AI models is 'unethical'
item.description: Australian artists have already had their music taken to train overseas AI models without payment or consent.

item.title: Protests scrutinised as antisemitism royal commission focuses on university activism
item.description: Universities have apologised to Jewish staff and students who have spoken of their distress at being targeted by protest movement.

item.title: Platform connects city investors with farming across Australia via their smartphones
item.description: An Australian investment platform is helping farmers raise capital by connecting them with everyday investors who can buy shares in crops and livestock while sharing the risks and rewards of farming.

item.title: Sub-Antarctic petrels spotted live off NSW coast for first time
item.description: Two Southern Ocean deepwater seabirds, the Kerguelen petrel and the grey petrel, have been photographed off Port Macquarie, during a "stunningly rare" southern seabird event.

item.title: Brenda Fricker, Oscar-winning Irish actress and Home Alone star, dies aged 81
item.description: Brenda Fricker became the first Irish actress to win an Oscar for the 1989 film My Left Foot and also played the kindly Pigeon Lady in Home Alone 2.

item.title: Queensland government crime statistics compare 'apples to oranges' amid timeframe inconsistency
item.description: Queensland's premier says new statistics show crime is falling across the state, but his data sources are inconsistent.

item.title: Artist behind portrait in Epstein files reunites with collector who purchased first piece
item.description: A high school art portrait launched Petrina Ryan-Kleid's career. Forty years and several satirical portraits later, she has been reunited with the person who bought her first work.

item.title: Four men refused bail over alleged home invasion, child abduction in Muswellbrook
item.description: Four men have been refused bail in a New South Wales court where they have been charged over an alleged June home invasion and abduction of two children, aged four and seven.

item.title: Attacking the media for asking legitimate question is straight out of Trump's playbook
item.description: A minister of the Crown's scolding of a journalist asking a legitimate question about firearm ownership, and the subsequent pile-on allowed to run on social media, should ring alarm bells for anyone concerned with democracy.

item.title: Winter weather brings new and unique shells to southern WA beaches
item.description: WA's southern coastline is covered in seashells, much to the delight of collectors. However, the state has a bag limit on how many you can take home.

item.title: How a century-old town hall became the heart of a small Tasmanian community
item.description: Every Sunday, a group of men and women get together for some American clogging, home-baked afternoon tea and community connection.

item.title: Lack of apprentices and economic pressures force butcher shops to close in outback Queensland
item.description: Outback Queensland is looking down the barrel of empty butcher shops, as multiple businesses close their doors, with no-one stepping in to keep selling the steaks.

item.title: Victoria on high alert for signs of deadly H5N1 bird flu
item.description: Victorians are on high alert for the deadly H5N1 bird flu, as citizen scientists help to monitor infected birds.

item.title: Young Australians with bowel cancer say doctors missing the warning signs
item.description: Georgie Cauchi had blood in her stool, constant pain and was struggling to get out of bed, but still in her 20s doctors told her she was too young to have bowel cancer. When it was finally discovered several years later, the tumour had grown so large it was blocking her entire bowel.

item.title: Harp therapist serenades lemurs, rhinos and fish at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium
item.description: Harp therapist Alison Ware provides musical enrichment for animals at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium, adapting what she plays to suit different species.

item.title: Mother feared for daughter's safety before regulator ordered childcare centre to close
item.description: A Canberra mother had concerns for her daughter's safety at Edge Early Learning in Higgins long before the ACT regulator ordered the centre to close.

item.title: Irish national admits role in 'nightmare' plan to help alleged criminal flee Australia
item.description: After agreeing to help smuggle an alleged drug trafficker out of Australia, Stephen Roche found himself at the centre of a living "nightmare", stranded in the outback without funds or friends, court documents have revealed.

item.title: Bridge destroyed in Iran's Bandar Khamir after US strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: US targets bridges, Iran hits Bahrain power plant as war threatens to expand in scope
item.description: Washington and Tehran have been testing the limits of escalation since their ceasefire agreement collapsed last week, raising the prospect of a return to all-out war.

item.title: Smoke risk remains for World Cup final as Donald Trump threatens Canada with tariffs
item.description: Fears that wildfire smoke could disrupt the World Cup final remain, as US President Donald Trump threatens Canada with tariffs.

item.title: Tasmanian government may have to pay $200m for Liberty Bell Bay remediation
item.description: The clean-up bill for the Liberty Bell Bay manganese smelter site may ultimately may be paid by the Tasmanian government, according to legal experts, following administrators' failure to secure a buyer for the embattled operation.

item.title: What comes next after the landmark BHP strike in Port Hedland?
item.description: For the first time in more than two decades, BHP workers in the the Pilbara went on strike. What do we know about how much impact it had and what comes next?

item.title: Victoria Police to host public meeting about investigation into alleged murder of Layla Jeffery
item.description: Victoria Police will hold a public meeting in Donald on Monday to update locals on their investigation into the alleged murder of 13-year-old Layla Jeffery.

item.title: Families reconnect to celebrate Carnarvon's landmark role in Australia's first live telecast
item.description: Carnarvon played host to Australia's first-ever live telecast with London 60 years ago. Now, one couple has visited the Pilbara town to recreate the family's involvement in the event.

item.title: How far can you go when defending your home against an intruder in NSW?
item.description: A suspected home invasion in northern NSW that left one man shot and an elderly couple with stab wounds sparks renewed discussion about self-defence and so-called "castle law".

item.title: Shark bites diver's forearm in waters off Bruny Island, Tasmania
item.description: A shark has bitten a diver on the forearm in waters off Bruny Island, Tasmania.

item.title: Australia's Oldyroos host over-70s football world cup
item.description: Around 90 of Australia's finest older footballers will play for the world title on home soil in October this year.

item.title: Australian government warns about risk of methanol poisoning and crime in Laos
item.description: Authorities say tourists should exercise a high degree of caution in the country due to the risk of methanol poisoning, which killed Australian women Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles in 2024.

item.title: China sitting on huge stockpiles of frozen beef as new tariffs bring imports to a halt
item.description: As imports from Australia hit a wall because of large tariffs, China turns to its historically high stockpile of frozen beef.

item.title: Lettuce at Taco Bell in five US states confirmed as a source of 'explosive diarrhoea'-causing parasite
item.description: A record number of cyclospora cases have been reported in more than 30 US states, but experts say not every illness is likely to have been caused by a single source.

item.title: England and France prepare for World Cup 3rd place game with Golden Boot in play
item.description: It is the World Cup game nobody wants to play in, but France and England say they are taking the third-placed play-off seriously.

item.title: Pies get home against Blues, Lions survive Eagles scare
item.description: Collingwood completes a 21-point win over rivals Carlton, as Brisbane is forced to work for a 24-point victory over West Coast.

item.title: Activists fight to preserve historic Frank Street home on Lismore flood plain
item.description: There is a push to save precious memories stored within the walls of an historic home on Lismore's flood plain.

item.title: Olympians return to iconic North Sydney pool for 50-year reunion of Montreal games
item.description: Members of the 1976 Australian Olympic swim team return to North Sydney Olympic pool to celebrate their 50-year reunion and revisit the venue that shaped their careers.

item.title: Man charged over law firm fire and further offences after a tobacco store shooting in Melbourne's west
item.description: Police have made an arrest over an alleged arson in Spotswood and laid further charges over a shooting outside a tobacco store in Altona North.

item.title: India reopens one of world's oldest nuclear power plants and that's just the beginning
item.description: Despite repeated setbacks and isolation, India continues to hold onto its nuclear dream.

item.title: Australian handcuffed by ICE officers in Las Vegas airport
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'It's not right': Dragons fume after pushing Warriors
item.description: Second-placed New Zealand Warriors squeak to a nervy 20-12 victory over wooden-spoon favourite St George Illawarra, although the Dragons fume over narrow refereeing calls.

item.title: Australian handcuffed by ICE officers in Las Vegas airport
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Perth medical researchers working on artificial placentas for premature babies
item.description: The aim of the technology is to give babies born too soon a few more weeks to grow and develop in womb-like conditions, and a better chance of a long and healthy life.

item.title: 'Jimothy', a raccoon with short spine syndrome who roams Seattle, gains cult following online
item.description: A raccoon with a rare birth defect has amassed a legion of fans after a video of it darting across a footpath and running up a set of stairs racked up more than 6.8 million views in just three days.

item.title: Deformed raccoon gains cult following after online videos go viral
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Wallabies thrash Italy as Schmidt era ends in triumph
item.description: The Wallabies record a convincing 57-10 victory over Italy in Perth, as Joe Schmidt's coaching tenure finishes on a winning note.

item.title: India's Skyroot takes off in first private orbital rocket mission
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: India's Skyroot launches Vikram-1 in nation's first private orbital rocket mission
item.description: India's first privately developed orbital rocket, the Vikram-1, has successfully lifted off on its maiden orbital mission.

item.title: Pauline Hanson delivers CPAC London speech to mostly empty seats
item.description: Pauline Hanson has ended her trip to Britain and Europe on a muted note, after about 120 people attended her speech at a political conference in London on Saturday.

item.title: Eight killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian targets
item.description: Two sprawling warehouses of Russia's major online retailer are hit by Ukrainian drones, local officials say, with massive plumes of smoke towering over a facility at Elektrostal, 50 kilometres east of Moscow.

item.title: Police in India remove activist amid hunger strike for education reform
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The Lemon Twigs on meeting Brian Wilson, making new album Look For Your Mind!
item.description: These New York twenty-somethings make music indebted to Brian Wilson, Todd Rundgren and the Fab Four. But do The Lemon Twigs get sick of the comparisons?

item.title: Put your music knowledge to the test in our Spicks and Specks quiz
item.description: Your spelling and general trivia skills are useless here. Settle into some pop culture nostalgia.

item.title: Australia's only professional penny-farthing maker has cult following
item.description: Farmer-turned-professional penny-farthing maker Dan Bolwell has a cult following of international enthusiasts.

item.title: How 'expansive masculinity' is helping boys become healthy men
item.description: These men are being the role models they never had. They hope to curb Australia's masculinity crisis.

item.title: Quiet change to Nauru's policing laws could impact NZYQ legal challenges
item.description: Advocates question the timing of law changes that coincide with mounting federal court challenges from individuals fighting deportation.

item.title: As Europe and Canada follow Australia's social media ban for young people, the US moves another way
item.description: More and more countries are following Australia's lead and introducing social media bans for children, but US policies are piecemeal and vary from state to state.

item.title: Indonesian corruption investigators discover stash of gold bars in hidden safe
item.description: Indonesian police investigating suspected corruption discover millions of dollars worth of currency and 74 kilograms of gold in a safe hidden in a luxury house near Jakarta.

item.title: How the World Cup trophy was designed and why the Jules Rimet is no longer awarded
item.description: It has become the most iconic trophy in world sport, and it all started in a small design studio in Milan more than 50 years ago.

item.title: Pauline Hanson risks losing everyday Australians while busy with the richest
item.description: Many voters are unhappy with the hand they feel successive governments have dealt them and no-one has tapped into that better than Pauline Hanson.

item.title: Biohacking and anti-aging science are raising hopes of living longer. What's hype and what shows promise?
item.description: The fear of aging is driving a wave of biohacking businesses promising to help restore youth and fight disease. Four Corners investigates the race to reverse the aging process.

item.title: Liberty Justice Center, the conservative law firm that took on Donald Trump and won
item.description: When a law centre decided to fight Donald Trump's "liberation day" tariffs, it was acutely aware of possible "retaliation" from a president who takes matters "personally".

item.title: US military launches new air strikes to 'swiftly punish' Iran for deaths of US troops
item.description: The US and Iran have intensified attacks since an interim ceasefire deal signed a month ago fell apart, raising the possibility of a return to all-out war.

item.title: Cattle farmers plant trees for sustainable aviation fuel
item.description: A north Queensland farming family is trialling planting biofuel-producing trees alongside their cattle.

item.title: Retired police returning to duty to fill Victoria Police shortages
item.description: Hundreds of former officers have applied to fill recruitment shortages with Victoria Police in a move aimed at releasing officers "trapped" at their desks.

item.title: World Cup: Lionel Messi received like royalty, but no-one matches the Knicks
item.description: Argentina's Lionel Messi has received rock star reception in New York City ahead of the World Cup final against Spain, but how much of a lasting impact will this tournament make in a crowded sporting market?

item.title: England wins crazy 10-goal thriller against France to claim third
item.description: England claims third place at the World Cup in a crazy game against France, which finishes 6-4 and sees Kylian Mbappe become, for now, the World Cup's greatest ever goalscorer.

item.title: From 40 cartographers to two: street directories nearing end of the road
item.description: While sales of the once-ubiquitous street directories have fallen dramatically since their peak in the 1980s, they still have a niche market.

item.title: Jazz festivals canned in Newcastle and Victoria over payday superannuation
item.description: Two long-running Australian jazz festivals have been cancelled, with organisers blaming new superannuation rules.

item.title: Lucas Herbert makes miracle shot at The Open but falls to equal fourth
item.description: Australia's Lucas Herbert makes one of the shots of The Open, but loses his day two lead to fall to fourth.

item.title: Barmah sand slug fix a complex headache due to competing demands
item.description: The Murray Darling Basin Authority is finalising its study on what to do about an increasing river impediment amid a First Nations call to find common ground.

item.title: From escaping Boggo Road Gaol to Boy Swallows Universe. The story of Arthur 'Slim' Halliday
item.description: In 1946, Arthur 'Slim' Halliday recruited two associates, scaled the walls of Boggo Road Gaol for a second time, hopped in a taxi and vanished.

item.title: Inquiry hears of CFMEU's political influence and intimidation in Queensland
item.description: The inquiry heard claims former senior public servants and industry figures faced aggressive union tactics.

item.title: Rory McIlroy says he is 'not particularly fond' of Bryson DeChambeau after The Open drama
item.description: Golf champion Rory McIlroy takes aim at Bryson DeChambeau after the American causes a delay in tee times being released.

item.title: Small bars revitalise Sydney nightlife as special entertainment precincts expand
item.description: Taran Howard opened up his small bar in Sydney's inner west last September. Since then, he has learnt how to mix drinks "on the fly".

item.title: Afghan women's refugee cricket team celebrates ICC funding, pathway to internationals
item.description: Five years since fleeing Afghanistan after the Taliban returned to power, the country's women's refugee cricket team has secured a pathway to compete at the international level.

item.title: Energy reliability scheme is adding to power bills, SA government admits
item.description: A new SA government energy scheme set to collect almost $44 million from electricity users this financial year has been described by a critic as a "hidden charge".

item.title: Giant earthworms found in SA 'almost certainly' a new species
item.description: A South Australian ecologist has been tracking giant earthworm sightings for 30 years, despite the species not officially existing in the state.

item.title: Tadej Pogačar claims another Tour de France stage as history beckons for Slovenian
item.description: Tadej Pogačar wins stage 14 of the Tour de France and increases his overall lead as a fifth overall victory looms.

item.title: Despite barely any population growth, Tasmania doesn't have enough houses
item.description: Despite having the slowest growing population in the country, Tasmania is still battling rising homelessness and an ever-tightening rental market.

item.title: Scotland's Josh Kerr breaks 27-year-old mile record at London Diamond League
item.description: Scotland's Josh Kerr stuns the crowd by breaking the mile record, as Australia's women shine at the Diamond League meet in London.

item.title: Police investigating fatal Ferrari crash, man dead, woman in hospital in West Gippsland
item.description: The woman, aged in her 70s, was flown to the Alfred Hospital with life-threatening injuries.

item.title: Social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami
item.description: British prosecutors say they are seeking the extradition of the Tate brothers from the US on rape and sex trafficking charges.

item.title: Fishers frustrated by new zoning restrictions in WA marine park
item.description: New zoning rules for a marine park off WA's far north are being rolled out, introducing fishing restrictions and penalties.

item.title: Iran shares video claiming attacks launched on US targets
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aussie Leo with growth hormone deficiency dreaming to star at World Cup like Messi
item.description: Lionel Messi was diagnosed with growth hormone deficiency, which is a rare condition, when he was younger. He has become a role model for young Leo Slattery from Brisbane.

item.title: Kimi Antonelli dominates to claim pole at Belgian F1 Grand Prix, Oscar Piastri starts sixth
item.description: Mercedes star Kimi Antonelli survives a clever Red Bull tactic to claim pole position at the Formula One Belgian Grand Prix.

item.title: NT Police officer fires two shots 'in an attempt to stop a vehicle' in Palmerston
item.description: A Northern Territory police officer has fired two shots at a vehicle while responding to a reported armed break-in in Palmerston.

item.title: China cracks down on AI companions, forcing millions to break up with virtual partners
item.description: A boyfriend betrayed 17-year-old Xiaoxue, so she made an AI partner. But now the government is trying to end her relationship.

item.title: Researcher says pesticides could be harming Carnaby's black cockatoos
item.description: Research has found pesticide levels "well above" maximums set for humans and animal feed, in some cases hundreds of times higher than set by regulators.

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Kylian Mbappé, Jude Bellingham see records tumble, and France's battle for third
item.description: The World Cup third-place play-off between England and France delivers absolute chaos as the goals fly in and the records tumble in Miami. Here are the quick hits.

item.title: Mitch McConnell's absence from the US Senate reignites debate about age limits
item.description: Washington's rumour mill is hyperactive at the best of times. But an elderly senator's lengthy absence from Congress is cranking it into frenzied overdrive.

item.title: Man dies after police pursuit across Queensland and northern NSW
item.description: NSW Police say a man involved in a pursuit after crossing the NSW-Qld border was armed with a modified lever action long arm.

item.title: Senator Andrew Bragg defies Liberal president Tony Abbott's 'failed' multiculturalism claim
item.description: Senator Andrew Bragg says he "strongly" disagrees with the recently elected Liberal Party president, who claims diversity is "not a strength".

item.title: WA's north has questions over policies implemented by outgoing MP Paul Papalia
item.description: Residents in WA's north have been left with a slew of unanswered questions relating to crime and justice in the wake of long-serving WA government minister Paul Papalia's resignation.

item.title: Love story: Why these best friends plan on being buried side by side
item.description: Tania Differding and Ella Crofts met as teenagers and have been inseparable since. The best friends, now aged 43, are planning the rest of their lives around one another.

item.title: World Cup final: Lamine Yamal, Spain's golden boy on the verge of teenage immortality
item.description: As Spain takes the field in the World Cup final, it will be led by a teenage sensation like few we have ever seen. This is the Lamine Yamal story so far.

item.title: The oil crisis may depend on China: here's why
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Moment Andrew Tate was arrested and handcuffed in Miami
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hinkley set to coach Devils, Caddy taken to hospital as Dons end drought
item.description: Tasmania prepares to unveil Ken Hinkley as its inaugural AFL coach as Essendon snaps a 12-game losing streak despite losing Nate Caddy early.

item.title: Bek left a doomsday church at 27. Here's what her life looks like now
item.description: Author Bek Condello says she has not spoken to her friends or family inside the church since she decided to leave.

item.title: Tom Trbojevic gutted after another injury, Titans, Dolphins record wins
item.description: The Dolphins shook off last week's humiliating thrashing to power past the Cowboys 36-16 at Lang Park, after Tom Trbojevic suffered another hamstring injury in Manly's defeat to Gold Coast.

item.title: Emu Run Tours sues vehicle manufacturer over fatal 2022 bus crash outside Alice Springs
item.description: An Alice Springs tour company is suing a vehicle manufacturer for negligence and breach of contract following a bus crash that killed a 69-year-old man in 2022.

item.title: Actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90 after more than half a century on Australian screens
item.description: Jason Donovan has paid tribute to his father who has died at the age of 90 after decades on Australian screens.

item.title: Woman fighting for life after crash in Catani
item.description: A woman, aged in her 20s, has suffered life-threatening injuries after the car her family was in rolled into a drain at Catani.

item.title: Secret Harbour by-election: Meet the candidates vying for your vote
item.description: The Secret Harbour by-election is shaping up as one of the most closely-watched political contests in WA for some time. Here's everything you need to know about the candidates and how to vote.

item.title: Argentina fans celebrate in Times Square ahead of World Cup final
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: World Cup: Argentina fans stage staggering Times Square takeover ahead of final against Spain
item.description: Times Square has been the epicentre for World Cup celebrations throughout the tournament, but it reached new heights as Argentina fans swamped the "centre of the universe" ahead of the final against Spain in nearby New Jersey.

item.title: Store-bought vodka from licensed IGA retailer found to contain key ingredient in paint stripper
item.description: Australia's bootleg booze market is booming and an alarming new discovery from a bottle of vodka sold in Melbourne reveals it is turning into a major health problem.

item.title: Australian boxer Tina Rahimi on praying, punching and fighting in the face of Islamophobia
item.description: Ahead of her professional debut, Tina Rahimi sat down for a lengthy interview about representation, the Islamophobia she encounters, and re-learning her identity after an Olympics heartbreak.

item.title: Allan government proposes new laws to target big tech and online trolls
item.description: Jacinta Allan is pledging nation-leading laws forcing tech giants to reveal the identity of trolls, and make it easier for parents to sue tech giants that cause psychiatric harm to their children.

item.title: Hundreds of activists join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of Pine Gap protest
item.description: Hundreds of activists and academics have gathered in Alice Springs to join Arrernte traditional owners ahead of a planned protest against the joint Australian-US military base at Pine Gap.

item.title: Sandy Brondello suspended by WNBA for inappropriate comment to Angel Reese
item.description: Australian coach Sandy Brondello apologises to WNBA star Angel Reese after she is suspended for one game by the league.

item.title: Special guest dazzles aspiring young astronauts at Adelaide's Space Day Out
item.description: Young South Australians got one step closer to achieving their space exploration dreams on Sunday when they met a "real-life" astronaut.

item.title: Russia bombards Kyic with ballistic missiles
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Russia unleashes one of biggest ballistic missile attacks of the Ukraine war, Kyiv says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Russia battered Ukraine's capital Kyiv overnight into Sunday local time, with one of its biggest ballistic missile barrages of the war.
